platform_overview.md::s1.heading: What Lamla Is
platform_overview.md::s1.text:
Lamla AI is an AI-powered learning platform that helps students study smarter and ace their exams. Upload your study materials and transform them into interactive quizzes and flashcards, or chat with an AI tutor available 24/7.

Core features:
- AI Tutor: Get instant explanations and personalized study guidance anytime
- Quiz Generator: Create practice quizzes from your study materials with automatic scoring
- Flashcards: Generate flashcard decks with built-in spaced repetition tracking
- Materials Library: Share and download community study resources
- Progress Dashboard: Track your learning activity and performance over time

platform_overview.md::s2.heading: Who Built Lamla
platform_overview.md::s2.text: Lamla AI is being built by a team of Computer Science and Information Technology students from KNUST (Kwame Nkrumah University of Science and Technology), Ghana. The team's goal is to make learning more intentional, engaging, and effective by giving students practical AI tools that support better study habits, clearer understanding, and more active revision.

platform_overview.md::s3.heading: Main Navigation
platform_overview.md::s3.text:
The main navigation menu is at the top of every page:
- Home: Landing page with platform information
- AI Tutor: Chat interface to ask the AI study assistant questions
- Quiz: Create and take practice quizzes
- Flashcards: Manage and study your flashcard decks
- Dashboard: View your study stats and activity
- Login/Logout: Access your account

Click any menu item to navigate to that feature.

platform_overview.md::s4.heading: Who It Is For
platform_overview.md::s4.text:
Lamla AI is designed for:
- University and college students preparing for exams
- High school students reviewing course material
- Independent learners mastering new subjects
- Anyone needing AI-powered study assistance

platform_overview.md::s5.heading: Key Benefits
platform_overview.md::s5.text:
Lamla AI helps you:
- Save time: Auto-generate quizzes and flashcards from your notes instead of manual creation
- Study smarter: Get AI explanations for concepts you do not understand
- Track progress: See your improvement and identify weak areas
- Learn actively: Practice with questions rather than passive reading
- Study anywhere: Access your materials and AI tutor from any device

platform_overview.md::s6.heading: Getting Started
platform_overview.md::s6.text:
1. Click Signup in the top navigation
2. Create your account with email and password
3. Verify your email address (check your inbox)
4. Log in and start using features
5. Navigate using the top menu to access different tools

platform_overview.md::s7.heading: Quick Access URLs and Website
platform_overview.md::s7.text:
The Lamla AI platform is live at: https://lamla-ai.vercel.app

Full page URLs:
- Homepage: https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/
- Login page: https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/auth/login
- Signup page: https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/auth/signup
- AI Tutor chat: https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/ai-tutor
- Create Quiz: https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/quiz/create
- Take Quiz: https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/quiz/play
- Quiz Results: https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/quiz/results
- Flashcard Decks: https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/flashcards
- Create Flashcard Deck: https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/flashcards/create
- Study a Deck: https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/flashcards/study/:id
- Materials Library: https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/materials
- Upload Material: https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/materials/upload
- Dashboard: https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/dashboard
- Profile settings: https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/profile

learning_tools.md::s1.heading: Quiz Generator
learning_tools.md::s1.text:
The Quiz Generator is at https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/quiz/create

How to create a quiz:
1. Go to the Quiz Creator page
2. Select a subject from the dropdown (Mathematics, Computer Science, Engineering, Biology, Chemistry, Physics, English, History, Geography, Economics, or Other)
3. Add your study content using one of three tabs:
   - Text Content tab: paste text (30 to 50,000 characters)
   - File Upload tab: upload PDF, DOCX, PPTX, or TXT (max 10MB)
   - YouTube tab: paste a YouTube URL and load transcript
4. Configure settings: Number of MCQ questions (max 20), Number of Short Answer questions (max 10), Quiz Time limit in minutes, Difficulty Level (Beginner, Intermediate, or Exam), optional Custom Instructions (max 1,500 chars)
5. Click Generate Quiz
6. You will be redirected to take the quiz at https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/quiz/play

After finishing:
- Click Submit Quiz
- See your results and score at https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/quiz/results

Quiz History: View all past quizzes in Dashboard under the Past Quizzes tab.

Extra: The Clear button resets all fields. Content is pre-filled when coming from the Materials Library Use for Quiz button. On the YouTube tab, the video appears embedded after transcript loads so you can confirm the correct video.

learning_tools.md::s2.heading: Flashcards
learning_tools.md::s2.text:
The Flashcard Decks page is at https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/flashcards

The page shows your existing decks with: deck title, number of cards, cards due today, and buttons: Study, View Deck, Delete.

Creating a new deck:
1. Click Create Deck button
2. Go to https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/flashcards/create
3. Enter study content via Text Content tab or File Upload tab
4. Choose number of cards (1 to 25), difficulty level, optional custom instructions
5. Click Generate Flashcards
6. Review generated cards, add deck title and subject
7. Click Save Deck

Viewing a deck:
- Click View Deck on any deck card to go to https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/flashcards/deck/:id
- See all cards, edit or delete individual cards
- Study button to start a review session

Study mode (https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/flashcards/study/:id):
1. Card shows question side first
2. Click to flip and see the answer
3. Rate yourself: Again (show sooner), Hard (moderate interval), Good (normal interval), Easy (longest interval)
4. Spaced repetition algorithm schedules future reviews

learning_tools.md::s3.heading: AI Tutor
learning_tools.md::s3.text:
The AI Tutor is at https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/ai-tutor (also works: /chat, /chatbot, /ai)

The chat interface shows:
- Greeting message from the AI
- Conversation history scrolling vertically
- User messages on the right, AI responses on the left
- Real-time streaming: AI text appears as it is generated

Input area at the bottom:
- Text box to type your question
- File attachment button (clip/folder icon): Upload a document to ask about
- Search mode button (globe icon): Enable web search for current info
- Send button (paper plane icon): Submit your message

How to use:
- Type a question and press Enter or click Send
- Attach a PDF, DOCX, PPTX, or TXT file (max 10MB) to ask questions about its content
- Enable web search mode to get answers based on current internet results

Features:
- Copy button to copy AI responses
- Clear history to start fresh
- Chats are auto-saved
- No login required for guests
- AI remembers context within the conversation

learning_tools.md::s4.heading: Materials Library
learning_tools.md::s4.text:
The Materials Library is at https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/materials

The page shows community-shared PDFs with: title, description, subject badge, uploader name, upload date, file size, download count.

Each material card has:
- Download button
- Use for Quiz button (magic wand icon): Extracts text and opens Quiz Creator with content pre-filled
- Delete button (only if you uploaded it or you are an admin)

Left sidebar filters: Search box and subject filters (Mathematics, Sciences, Engineering, Computing, Humanities, Business, Languages, Medicine, Law, Arts, Other)

Uploading materials:
1. Click Upload Material button (top right, requires login)
2. Go to https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/materials/upload
3. Fill in: Title, Description, Subject, and select a PDF file
4. Click Upload
5. Material is added to the library and visible to everyone

Managing your uploads:
- View uploads in Dashboard under the Materials tab
- Delete materials you no longer want to share
- See download counts on your materials

learning_tools.md::s5.heading: Dashboard And Progress
learning_tools.md::s5.text:
The Dashboard is at https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/dashboard

Left sidebar navigation: Dashboard tab (overview), Past Quizzes tab, Materials tab, Profile tab, Logout button.

Overview tab shows 4 statistics cards:
1. Total Quizzes: number of quizzes completed
2. Average Score: average quiz score percentage
3. Study Streak: consecutive days active
4. Flashcard Sets: number of flashcard decks created

Weak Areas card: Shows your lowest-accuracy topics (if you have taken quizzes). Each has a Practice button to start a focused quiz on that topic.

Quick Actions on the dashboard:
- Create Quiz: https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/quiz/create
- Flashcards: https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/flashcards
- AI Tutor: https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/ai-tutor

Recent Activity section: Timeline of quiz completions with scores, flashcard decks created, materials uploaded.

accounts_and_auth.md::s1.heading: Sign Up And Login
accounts_and_auth.md::s1.text:
Signup page: https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/auth/signup
Login page: https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/auth/login

To create an account:
1. Click Signup in the top navigation menu
2. Fill in: Email address (used as your login), Username (your display name), Password
3. Click the Sign Up button
4. You will be redirected to verify your email

Alternatively sign up with Google:
1. Click Continue with Google on the signup page
2. Select your Google account
3. Account is created instantly, no email verification needed
4. You are redirected to your dashboard immediately

To log in:
1. Click Login in the top navigation menu
2. Enter your email and password
3. Click Sign in or press Enter
4. You are redirected to your Dashboard

accounts_and_auth.md::s2.heading: Email Verification
accounts_and_auth.md::s2.text:
After signing up you will see a verification page at https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/auth/verify-email

Steps:
1. Check your email inbox for a verification message from Lamla AI
2. Click the link in the email
3. Your account is now verified and fully active

If you do not receive the email:
- Check your spam or junk folder
- Wait a few minutes, emails can be delayed
- Look for a Resend verification email button on the verification page

accounts_and_auth.md::s3.heading: Profile Settings
accounts_and_auth.md::s3.text:
Profile settings page: https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/profile (also accessible from Dashboard sidebar under Profile tab)

Account Information:
- Edit your Username (max 50 characters, must be unique) and Email Address
- Click Save Changes when done (button is only enabled when changes are detected)

Profile Photo:
1. Find the Profile Photo section
2. Click the file input and select an image
3. Supported formats: JPEG, PNG, WebP, GIF
4. Maximum size: 5 MB
5. Click Upload Photo
6. See success message: Photo updated

Your photo appears on the Profile page and across the platform. If no photo is uploaded, your first username letter is shown as an avatar.

accounts_and_auth.md::s4.heading: Dashboard Access
accounts_and_auth.md::s4.text:
Dashboard URL: https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/dashboard

Features:
- Overview tab: Statistics cards, weak areas card, and quick actions
- Past Quizzes tab: View quiz history
- Materials tab: Materials you have uploaded
- Profile tab: Account settings

accounts_and_auth.md::s5.heading: Logout
accounts_and_auth.md::s5.text:
To log out:
1. Click the Logout button in the top navigation menu
2. You will be logged out and returned to the home page
3. Your session ends securely

accounts_and_auth.md::s6.heading: Password Security
accounts_and_auth.md::s6.text:
Security features:
- Passwords are encrypted and never visible
- Show/hide password toggle on the login page (eye icon)
- Secure login sessions
- Rate limiting prevents brute-force attacks

To change your password: Access your profile settings when logged in.
Forgot password: Use the password reset option on the login page at https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/auth/login

files_and_limits.md::s1.heading: Supported Upload Types
files_and_limits.md::s1.text:
Supported file types for upload:
- PDF (.pdf): Textbooks, notes, research papers, study guides
- Microsoft Word (.docx): Essays, notes, documents
- Microsoft PowerPoint (.pptx): Lecture slides, presentations
- Plain Text (.txt): Simple text notes

Where to upload files:
- Quiz Creator (https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/quiz/create): Click the File Upload tab
- Flashcard Creator (https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/flashcards/create): Click the File Upload tab
- AI Tutor Chat (https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/ai-tutor): Click the folder/clip icon button at the bottom
- Materials Library (https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/materials/upload): Upload Material page (PDF only for community sharing)

After upload, text is automatically extracted. For Quiz/Flashcards the extracted text fills the content field. For AI Tutor you can ask questions about the file. For Materials the PDF is shared publicly.

files_and_limits.md::s2.heading: File Size Limits
files_and_limits.md::s2.text:
Maximum file size: 10 MB

This applies to Quiz uploads, Flashcard uploads, AI Tutor file attachments, and Materials Library uploads.

If your file is too large:
- Split large documents into smaller sections
- Compress PDF files using online PDF tools
- Remove images or unnecessary pages
- Copy and paste text directly instead of uploading a file

Error message shown: File too large (max 10MB)

files_and_limits.md::s3.heading: Text Length Limits
files_and_limits.md::s3.text:
Maximum extracted text: 50,000 characters (approximately 8,000 to 10,000 words or 15 to 20 pages of dense text)

Quiz generation:
- Minimum: 30 characters
- Maximum: 50,000 characters
- Custom prompt: max 1,500 characters
- Questions per quiz: 1 to 25

Flashcard generation:
- Minimum: 30 characters
- Maximum: 50,000 characters
- Custom prompt: max 1,500 characters
- Cards per deck: 1 to 100 (1 to 25 per generation batch)

Flashcard editing:
- Question: 1 to 2,000 characters
- Answer: 1 to 4,000 characters

Subject and title fields: max 255 characters

files_and_limits.md::s4.heading: Request Timeouts
files_and_limits.md::s4.text:
AI processing requests have timeout limits.

Common timeout scenarios: very long documents, high AI provider load, network issues, complex generation requests.

What to do if you hit a timeout:
1. Retry the request
2. Reduce input size or use fewer questions
3. Split into multiple smaller requests
4. Simplify custom instructions
5. Try again later when the system is less loaded

files_and_limits.md::s5.heading: Model Context Limits
files_and_limits.md::s5.text:
AI models have maximum context windows. Very long documents may be truncated. Chat conversations have limited history retention. File uploads are processed in manageable chunks.

Best practices:
- Focus on relevant sections of long documents
- Ask specific questions rather than broad ones
- Break large study materials into topic-based sections

files_and_limits.md::s6.heading: Rate Limiting
files_and_limits.md::s6.text:
Some endpoints have rate limiting to ensure fair usage.

Protected endpoints:
- Login and signup (prevents brute-force attacks)
- Quiz generation (prevents abuse)
- Flashcard generation (manages AI costs)

If rate limited: wait for the cooldown period, then retry. Avoid rapid repeated submissions.

files_and_limits.md::s7.heading: Best Practices For File Uploads
files_and_limits.md::s7.text:
For best results with file uploads:
1. Keep files under 10MB, compress or split if needed
2. Use clear, well-formatted documents for better text extraction
3. Remove unnecessary pages and focus on relevant content
4. Choose the appropriate difficulty level for quiz/flashcard generation
5. Test with smaller files first to verify the format works
6. Use specific custom prompts to guide AI generation
7. Save generated content before creating more to avoid losing work

files_and_limits.md::s8.heading: Common Error Messages
files_and_limits.md::s8.text:
File upload errors:
- File too large (max 10MB): Exceeds size limit
- Failed to extract text: File may be image-based PDF or corrupted
- Unsupported file type: Use PDF, DOCX, PPTX, or TXT only

Content errors:
- Please enter at least 30 characters: Need more text to generate
- Text is too long (max 50,000): Reduce content length
- Please select a subject: Choose from dropdown or enter a custom subject

Generation errors:
- Generation failed: AI service temporarily unavailable, try again
- Service unavailable: Backend issue, retry in a moment

files_and_limits.md::s9.heading: Browser And Device Support
files_and_limits.md::s9.text:
Supported browsers: Chrome (recommended), Firefox, Safari, Microsoft Edge, mobile browsers.
Supported devices: Desktop computers, laptops, tablets, smartphones.

Best experience:
- Desktop or laptop for creating quizzes and flashcards
- Any device works well for AI Tutor chat
- Mobile-friendly interface throughout the platform

Troubleshooting browser issues:
- Try a different browser
- Clear browser cache and cookies
- Check your internet connection
- Disable browser extensions temporarily
- Update to the latest browser version

files_and_limits.md::s10.heading: YouTube Quiz Input
files_and_limits.md::s10.text:
You can generate quizzes directly from YouTube videos in Quiz Creator.

How to use:
1. Open https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/quiz/create
2. Select the YouTube tab
3. Paste a YouTube URL (supported: youtube.com/watch?v=..., youtu.be/..., youtube.com/shorts/...)
4. Click Load Transcript
5. After transcript loads, configure quiz settings and click Generate Quiz

Important limits and requirements:
- Captions must be available on the video (manual or auto-generated)
- Very long transcripts are trimmed before AI generation to keep quiz quality high
- If transcript loading fails, try another URL or a video with captions enabled

Common YouTube errors and fixes:
- Captions are disabled or unavailable: choose a video with captions
- Could not find a YouTube video ID: check URL format
- Transcript too short: choose a video with more spoken content
- Please load a YouTube transcript first: click Load Transcript before Generate

support_and_policies.md::s1.heading: How To Get Help
support_and_policies.md::s1.text:
Need help with Lamla? Contact our support team:

- Email: lamlaaiteam@gmail.com
- WhatsApp: +233509341251

Both channels are monitored by our team.

support_and_policies.md::s2.heading: What We Can Help With
support_and_policies.md::s2.text:
Our support team can assist with:

Account issues: cannot log in, forgot password, email verification problems, profile or settings questions, account security concerns.

Feature questions: how to use quizzes, flashcards, or AI Tutor, understanding the dashboard, navigating the platform, feature requests or suggestions.

Technical problems: files not uploading, quiz or flashcard generation errors, chat not responding, page loading issues, unexpected errors or bugs.

General inquiries: questions about how Lamla works, feedback about your experience, partnership or collaboration inquiries.

support_and_policies.md::s3.heading: Getting Faster Help
support_and_policies.md::s3.text:
To get faster support, include in your message:
- A clear description of the problem
- What you were trying to do
- Any error messages you saw (exact text or screenshot)
- Your device and browser
- Your account email if applicable

Before contacting support, try:
- Refreshing the page
- Checking file size limits (10MB max)
- Verifying your internet connection
- Trying a different browser

support_and_policies.md::s4.heading: Feedback And Suggestions
support_and_policies.md::s4.text:
Share feedback, suggestions, or ideas for new features by contacting us:
- Email: lamlaaiteam@gmail.com
- WhatsApp: +233509341251

models_and_capabilities.md::s1.heading: AI Behind Lamla
models_and_capabilities.md::s1.text:
Lamla is powered by state-of-the-art large language models including DeepSeek, Claude (Anthropic), and GPT4 (OpenAI).

These are the same class of models used by leading AI products worldwide. They handle quiz generation, flashcard creation, short-answer evaluation, and the AI Tutor chat.

For reliability, Lamla connects to multiple AI providers. If one is slow or unavailable, the system automatically switches to another — so you rarely experience downtime.

models_and_capabilities.md::s2.heading: Why Responses Sometimes Vary
models_and_capabilities.md::s2.text:
AI response quality and speed can vary because:
- Lamla uses multiple AI providers and automatically selects the best available one
- Provider performance fluctuates based on demand and availability
- Complex requests (long documents, many questions) take longer to process

If a response seems slow or lower quality than usual, try again — the system may route to a different provider on the next request.

learning_tools.md::s6.heading: Quiz History Page
learning_tools.md::s6.text:
Quiz History is at https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/quiz

This is a dedicated page for authenticated users showing all past quizzes. You must be logged in to access it — guests are redirected to login.

The page shows:
- All past quizzes with subject, score percentage, and date
- A 'Take a New Quiz' button (top right) to start a fresh quiz
- Sidebar navigation identical to the Dashboard

Accessing quiz history:
- Click Quiz in the top navigation (if logged in, goes directly to /quiz)
- Or navigate to https://lamla-ai.vercel.app/quiz

Note: Guests who are not logged in are redirected to the login page when trying to access quiz history.

learning_tools.md::s7.heading: Weak Area Detection and Spaced Quiz Scheduling
learning_tools.md::s7.text:
Lamla automatically tracks your performance per topic after every quiz you submit.

Weak Area Detection:
- After each quiz, Lamla records how many questions you got right and wrong for that topic
- Over time it builds a per-topic accuracy map (e.g. 'Thermodynamics: 38% accuracy across 18 questions')
- The Dashboard overview shows your weakest topics as a 'Weak Areas' card
- Each weak area has a 'Practice' button that opens Quiz Creator with that subject pre-filled
- Only topics with at least 3 questions attempted are shown (to avoid noise from a single quiz)
- The AI Tutor also knows your weak areas and can factor them into study suggestions

Spaced Quiz Scheduling:
- Lamla uses the SM-2 spaced repetition algorithm (also used for flashcards) to schedule quiz topics
- Topics you score well on are reviewed less often; topics you struggle with come back sooner
- 'Due for review' topics are tracked automatically — no action needed from you
- The AI Tutor can tell you which topics are due for review today

How to use it:
- Take quizzes regularly — data improves as you do more quizzes
- Check the Dashboard for your weak areas card
- Ask the AI Tutor: 'What should I study today?' or 'What are my weakest topics?'

learning_tools.md::s8.heading: I Know Your Progress
learning_tools.md::s8.text:
I am aware of your learning progress. When you are logged in and have taken at least one quiz, I automatically know:
- How many quizzes you have taken and your average score
- Your weakest topics (lowest accuracy across at least 3 questions)
- Which topics are due for review based on spaced repetition scheduling

Because I have this context, you can ask me things like:
- 'What should I study today?'
- 'What are my weakest topics?'
- 'Am I ready for my exam?'
- 'Which topic should I focus on next?'
- 'How am I doing overall?'

I use your actual performance data to give you specific, relevant answers — not generic advice.

This builds automatically as you take more quizzes. No setup needed.

If you are a guest or have not taken any quizzes yet, I do not have performance data for you — but I can still help with any subject questions you have.
